stories.title: India Celebrates Ram Navami Festival Across the Country
stories.summary: Ram Navami, the birthday of Lord Ram, is being celebrated with great joy across India on March 30, 2026. Temples are decorated with flowers and lights, and people are singing devotional songs and offering prayers. Grand processions called 'Shobha Yatras' are taking place in cities like Ayodhya, Varanasi, and Mumbai.

stories.title: Scientists Discover New Way to Track Ocean Health From Space
stories.summary: Researchers have announced an improved method of using satellites to measure the health of our oceans by tracking tiny plants called phytoplankton. These microscopic plants produce half of Earth's oxygen and are very important for sea life. Scientists say this new technology will help us understand climate change better.

stories.title: IPL 2026 Season Heats Up With Exciting Matches
stories.summary: The Indian Premier League cricket season is in full swing, with teams across India competing in thrilling matches. Fans are filling stadiums and watching on television to cheer for their favourite teams. Young cricket stars are getting a chance to shine alongside experienced international players.

stories.title: Tech Companies Race to Build Smarter AI Assistants
stories.summary: Big technology companies around the world are competing to make artificial intelligence helpers that can understand and answer questions even better than before. These AI tools can now help students with homework, help doctors find diseases, and even write computer programs. Experts say AI will keep getting smarter throughout 2026.

stories.title: World Leaders Meet to Discuss Climate Goals Progress
stories.summary: Government leaders from many countries gathered this week to review how well nations are doing at reducing pollution and fighting climate change. Many countries shared updates on their use of solar and wind energy, which has grown a lot in recent years. Children and young activists around the world are watching closely to see if leaders keep their promises.

infographic.fact1: Phytoplankton are tiny plant-like creatures that live in the ocean — so small you need a microscope to see them!
infographic.fact2: They produce about 50% of all the oxygen on Earth, which means every second breath you take comes from the ocean.
infographic.fact3: Billions of sea animals, from tiny shrimp to giant whales, depend on phytoplankton as the start of their food chain.
infographic.fact4: When oceans get warmer due to climate change, phytoplankton numbers can drop — which is why scientists use satellites to monitor them.

quiz.q: Which festival celebrating Lord Ram's birthday was observed on March 30, 2026?
What are the tiny ocean plants that scientists are now tracking from space?
Roughly how much of Earth's oxygen do ocean phytoplankton produce?
